codeReview.summary: The TrustPolicy and TrustResolver implementation fully covers the feature requirements: allow-list-based tool trust enforcement with clear error messages for untrusted tools. The implementation is clean, well-tested, and follows existing crate conventions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-worker skill requires invoking the Rust skill at the start, but the transcript skeleton shows the worker invoked 'mission-worker-base' instead. The skill says to invoke 'Rust' (not 'rust'). However, the worker did invoke rust-worker properly later in the transcript. This may be a minor procedural deviation that had no practical impact since the code is idiomatic Rust.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows Skill{message:{skill:mission-worker-base}} as first tool call. The rust-worker SKILL.md says 'Invoke the Rust skill (via Skill tool)' but does not specify a particular skill name format. The worker did follow the core TDD procedure (write tests first, then implement, then validate).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker created a new module file (trust_policy.rs) and an integration test file (tests/trust_resolution.rs) without needing to reference any documentation — the feature was straightforward and the existing patterns were clear. No knowledge gaps were observed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: No references to .factory/library/ files in transcript. Worker read existing code (registry.rs, executor.rs, lib.rs) to understand patterns before implementing.

summary: The obs-trust-resolution feature passes scrutiny. TrustPolicy (allow-list struct with trust_all(), allow(), allow_many()) and TrustResolver (check() and require_trusted()) are implemented correctly in trust_policy.rs with 17 unit tests and 2 integration tests covering all expected behavior. Error messages for untrusted tools are clear and descriptive. Code follows existing swell-tools conventions, uses thiserror for TrustError, and exports via lib.rs. All verifications passed (19 tests, clippy clean, full crate test suite).
